paper_title: FT-IR, XRD, SEM, TGA, Adsorption Mechanism, and Isotherm Study of CS-DE@CA Beads

methods.description: FT-IR and XRD were used to investigate the structural interactions, crosslinking, and crystal phase structure of CS-DE@CA.

steps.description: FT-IR was used for investigating the structural interactions and the existence of crosslinking on CS-DE@CA.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze FT-IR spectra for structural interactions and crosslinking
inputs: FT-IR spectra of DE, CS-DE, CS-DE@CA

steps.description: XRD patterns results of raw DE and CS-DE@CA were analyzed to prove the crystal phase structure of DE.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze XRD patterns for crystal phase structure
inputs: XRD patterns of raw DE, CS-DE@CA

methods.description: SEM was employed to explore the morphology and microstructure of DE, CS-DE, and CS-DE@CA beads.

steps.description: SEM was employed to further explore the morphology and microstructure of DE, CS-DE, and CS-DE@CA beads.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze SEM images for morphology and microstructure
inputs: SEM images of DE, CS-DE, CS-DE@CA

steps.description: EDX map showed that Si, O, C, and N were evenly distributed on the CS-DE.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze EDX map for elemental distribution

methods.description: TGA curves analysis was conducted to investigate the thermal stability of alginate matrix and the final CS-DE@CA beads.

steps.description: TGA curves of the CA and the CS-DE@CA beads was performed under N2 flow with heating rate of 20°C/min and up to 600°C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform TGA analysis for thermal stability

methods.description: Analysis of the possible adsorption mechanisms of Congo red on CS-DE@CA beads, including physical and chemical interactions.

steps.description: The interaction between Congo red molecules and adsorbent was dominated by physical behavior and chemical interactions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze adsorption mechanisms for Congo red

methods.description: Langmuir and Freundlich isotherm models were used to evaluate the adsorption effect of CS-DE@CA for Congo red.

steps.description: Langmuir and Freundlich isotherm models were used to fit the collected experiment parameter.
steps.searchable_summary: fit isotherm models to adsorption data
